We are pushing forward at floor-level along a hallway dimly daylit from the room in the background that it opens into. Music enters at the cut, an Italian tenor, singing opera. The music has some perspective: a record*playing in another apartment, perhaps, down an airshaft. A cat's feet enter frame and it leads the continuing push in. The cat enters the background room, camera keeping pace. The cat veers to one side bringing into frame the bottom of a sofa. The arm of someone above frame asleep on the couch lolls down onto the floor. We can hear the sleeper's heavy breathing. The cat leaps up, leaving frame.

Close on the sleeper: Llewyn Davis, lying on his back. At the sound of soft impact he lets out a startled grunt and his eyes open. He blinks. He looks down the length of his body, chin digging into chest. His point of view: the cat stands on his chest staring back, purring with rumble. loud rhythmic Llewyn raises a hand to swipe the cat away. As we hear the cat padding around the room we cut wider on Llewyn. swinging his feet out. He is in his undies. e stirs and rises, The walls in this den are decorated with masks and totems and other na1* e earlycivilization art. Llewyn sits gazing stupidly about for a beat. He reaches for pants, plops them into his lap. He look down at the end table just off the sofa arm. On it, some change nd three subway tokens, which he swipes into an open hand and dumps into one ants pocket. Also on the table, a wallet. He thumbs open the bill compartment. It co tains three dollars. Close on him as he looks, then flips the wallet shut and shoves it into an ther pocket.

We are lookfog down'.* the length of a darkish hallway giving onto the bri hter den. Lle~n':.$J1.~ad appears at the far end as he cranes to peer down the hall. He stares out for a still beat~
... Hello? No answer.
Llewyn relaxes, enters the hall, walks toward us in his underwear. The ckt crosses the room behind him.

Llewyn enters and bends with an oomph to pull his guitar out of a case. He sits on a sofa, experimenting idly with chords til he finds his way into the continuing opera. He accompanies it through a couple of chord changes, humming. He loudly clears phlegm.
KITCHEN

Eggs are cracked into a bowl. Wider: Llewyn, still undied, whisks the eggs. Afl1:r a long beat of vigorous whisking he looks about, pulls open a drawer, doesn't see what he is after. He looks around, lost, at all thereabinetry,;;:

Llewyn walks down the hal I forking scrambled egg from a plate to his mouth, idly looking at the pi..:tur~~ on the walls.

The plate of eggs, empty now, sits abandoned on the hi-fl cabinet next to him. He lands on a record, pulls it out. He looks at the sleeve with a half-smile, slips out the LP, puts it on the'hi-fi spindle.
MINUTES LATER

"Dink's Song" issues from the hi-fi, sung by a harmonizing male duo. Dressed now, wearing a corduroy sport coat, Llewyin is hunched at a sideboard, scribbling something onto scratch paper: Thanks for the couch. I was a sorry mess last night. He signs:

The hallway i a small space with only one other apartment door giving on to it~ there is also an eleva or. As Llewyn leaves, the cat tries to accompany him. A grunt from Llewyn. He tries awkwardly to hook the cat with a foot fails. The purring cat runs to the far end of the hallway. :it goes by; he Llewyn hops after it, setting down his guitar case. One step into pursuit e hears the apartment door close-solidly-behind him.

He turns back to the door, tries the knob which he knows will not turn. And it does not; lo~kc;d. . .. Goddamnit. The cat is winding ar01,md the legs of a small table in the hallway. While waiting, he pointlessly tries the first apartment door again. We hear the elevator arriving, a cage door being slid. The outer door is opened by the attendant. keep it tifthey get. back? I have to run the elevator.

Wider shows Llewyn standing in a phone booth with the handset wedge between shoulder and ear and one hand holding the address book and the other h gging the cat to his chest.
